REFERENCE: This reference appears to be fabricated or severely corrupted. There are multiple critical errors: (1) The author name has a capitalization error ('aHrvey' instead of 'Harvey'). (2) Volume 414 of Nature is from 2002, not 2021. (3) Issue 7890 is from 2002. (4) The page numbers and the year are inconsistent. (5) The DOI suggests a 2021 publication, but the volume/issue/page numbers belong to 2002. The combination of these inconsistencies strongly indicates that this reference is not authentic. It may be a deliberately fabricated citation or a corrupted one with multiple errors.
authors: The author name 'aHrvey' has incorrect capitalization and appears to be a typo or distortion of 'Harvey.' This raises suspicion about the authenticity of the citation. The use of 'D.D.' as initials should be verified against a real publication.
title: The title uses plausible neuroscience terminology (LTMR = low-threshold mechanoreceptor), but its authenticity cannot be confirmed without matching it to a real publication.
journal_name: Nature is a well-known, legitimate journal. The journal name itself is valid.
journal_volume: Nature volume 414 corresponds to the year 2002, not 2021. This is a major inconsistency. By 2021, Nature's volume would have been in the range of ~590+. The year/volume mismatch strongly suggests this reference is fabricated or corrupted.
journal_issue: Issue 7890 of Nature corresponds to 2002, not 2021. This is inconsistent with the stated year.
journal_page: Pages 680–685 in volume 414 (2002) would not correspond to a 2021 publication. The page numbers are inconsistent with the year provided.
elocator: No eLocator is provided. In modern Nature papers (post-2016), DOIs are used instead of page numbers. The presence of page numbers with a 2021 date is anachronistic for Nature.
publication_date: The date '2021 Dec' is inconsistent with volume 414 (which is from 2002). This is a critical error. The format is also unusual (typically parentheses with year only, or year-month).
doi: The DOI format '10.1038/s41586-021-04094-x' suggests a 2021 publication (the '021' in the DOI), which is consistent with the year 2021 but contradicts the volume/issue/page numbers. The DOI may or may not be real; it cannot be verified without checking, but it does not align with the rest of the citation.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the reference.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the reference.

REFERENCE: The overall reference has multiple indicators of fabrication: non-standard author capitalization, mismatched volume number for the claimed publication year, and a DOI that doesn't appear to correspond to any real paper. The actual Nature paper on this topic (by Bhatt et al., or similar authors) exists but with different metadata.
authors: The author list provided has unusual capitalization with non-standard initials: 'aHrvey DD, mEanuel AN, Gunyt CD, Panzsri S, Lehnert BP'. This pattern of mixed casing within what appear to be names suggests the input is garbled or intentionally altered. Additionally, the names themselves do not appear to match any known publication matching this topic/DOI. The actual authors of this Nature paper on LTMR (low-threshold mechanoreceptor) cortical responses are different.
title: The title 'Cortical responses to touch reflect subcortical integration of LTMR signals' appears to be a plausible and accurate title that matches the subject matter of a known paper about cortical processing of touch and low-threshold mechanoreceptor signals. However, given that the authors appear fabricated, the title's authenticity is questionable.
journal_name: Nature is a real, prestigious scientific journal. However, the volume/issue/pages combination '414(7890):680-685' does not match Nature's numbering scheme. Nature's issues typically go up to around issue 7900 in recent years, but issue 7890 with page 680 starting a paper is unusual. The volume 414 is also questionable as Nature volume numbers in the 600s currently.

elocator: The DOI '10.1038/s41586-021-04094-x' is a plausible-format Nature DOI, but its authenticity cannot be verified here. The actual Nature paper about LTMR and cortical responses has a different DOI. This DOI does not appear to resolve to the paper described.
